title: Config
description:
Top-level ZeroClaw configuration, loaded from `config.toml`.

Resolution order: `ZEROCLAW_WORKSPACE` env → `active_workspace.toml` marker → `~/.zeroclaw/config.toml`.

agents.description: Delegate agent configurations for multi-agent workflows.

api_key.description: API key for the selected provider. Overridden by `ZEROCLAW_API_KEY` or `API_KEY` env vars.

api_url.description: Base URL override for provider API (e.g. "http://10.0.0.1:11434" for remote Ollama)

channels_config.description: Channel configurations: Telegram, Discord, Slack, etc. (`[channels_config]`).

cost.description: Cost tracking and budget enforcement configuration (`[cost]`).

default_model.description: Default model routed through the selected provider (e.g. `"anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6"`).

default_provider.description: Default provider ID or alias (e.g. `"openrouter"`, `"ollama"`, `"anthropic"`). Default: `"openrouter"`.

embedding_routes.description: Embedding routing rules — route `hint:<name>` to specific provider+model combos.

gateway.description: Gateway server configuration: host, port, pairing, rate limits (`[gateway]`).

goal_loop.description: Goal loop configuration for autonomous long-term goal execution (`[goal_loop]`).

heartbeat.description: Heartbeat configuration for periodic health pings (`[heartbeat]`).

hooks.description: Hooks configuration (lifecycle hooks and built-in hook toggles).

identity.description: Identity format configuration: OpenClaw or AIEOS (`[identity]`).

memory.description: Memory backend configuration: sqlite, markdown, embeddings (`[memory]`).

model_providers.description: Optional named provider profiles keyed by id (Codex app-server compatible layout).

model_routes.description: Model routing rules — route `hint:<name>` to specific provider+model combos.

model_support_vision.description:
Vision support override for the active provider/model.
- `None` (default): use provider's built-in default
- `Some(true)`: force vision support on (e.g. Ollama running llava)
- `Some(false)`: force vision support off

peripherals.description: Peripheral board configuration for hardware integration (`[peripherals]`).

plugins.description: Plugin system configuration (discovery, loading, per-plugin config).

provider_api.description: Optional API protocol mode for `custom:` providers.

proxy.description: Proxy configuration for outbound HTTP/HTTPS/SOCKS5 traffic (`[proxy]`).

query_classification.description: Automatic query classification — maps user messages to model hints.

reliability.description: Reliability settings: retries, fallback providers, backoff (`[reliability]`).

research.description: Research phase configuration (`[research]`). Proactive information gathering.

runtime.description: Runtime adapter configuration (`[runtime]`). Controls native vs Docker execution.

scheduler.description: Scheduler configuration for periodic task execution (`[scheduler]`).

skills.description: Skills loading and community repository behavior (`[skills]`).

transcription.description: Voice transcription configuration (Whisper API via Groq).

tunnel.description: Tunnel configuration for exposing the gateway publicly (`[tunnel]`).

compact_context.description: When true: bootstrap_max_chars=6000, rag_chunk_limit=2. Use for 13B or smaller models.

max_history_messages.description: Maximum conversation history messages retained per session. Default: `50`.

max_tool_iterations.description:
Maximum tool-call loop turns per user message. Default: `20`.
Setting to `0` falls back to the safe default of `20`.

parallel_tools.description: Enable parallel tool execution within a single iteration. Default: `false`.

AgentsIpcConfig.description:
Inter-process agent communication configuration (`[agents_ipc]` section).

When enabled, registers IPC tools that let independent ZeroClaw processes
on the same host discover each other and exchange messages via a shared
SQLite database. Disabled by default (zero overhead when off).

db_path.description: Path to shared SQLite database (all agents on this host share one file).

staleness_secs.description: Agents not seen within this window are considered offline (seconds).

log_path.description: Path to audit log file (relative to zeroclaw dir)

max_size_mb.description: Maximum log size in MB before rotation

sign_events.description: Sign events with HMAC for tamper evidence

AutonomyConfig.description:
Autonomy and security policy configuration (`[autonomy]` section).

Controls what the agent is allowed to do: shell commands, filesystem access,
risk approval gates, and per-policy budgets.

allowed_commands.description: Allowlist of executable names permitted for shell execution.

allowed_roots.description:
Extra directory roots the agent may read/write outside the workspace.
Supports absolute, `~/...`, and workspace-relative entries.
Resolved paths under any of these roots pass `is_resolved_path_allowed`.

always_ask.description: Tools that always require interactive approval, even after "Always".

auto_approve.description: Tools that never require approval (e.g. read-only tools).

block_high_risk_commands.description: Block high-risk shell commands even if allowlisted.

forbidden_paths.description: Explicit path denylist. Default includes system-critical paths and sensitive dotdirs.

max_actions_per_hour.description: Maximum actions allowed per hour per policy. Default: `100`.

max_cost_per_day_cents.description: Maximum cost per day in cents per policy. Default: `1000`.

non_cli_approval_approvers.description:
Optional allowlist for who can manage non-CLI approval commands.

When empty, any sender already admitted by the channel allowlist can
use approval-management commands.

Supported entry formats:
- `"*"`: allow any sender on any channel
- `"alice"`: allow sender `alice` on any channel
- `"telegram:alice"`: allow sender `alice` only on `telegram`
- `"telegram:*"`: allow any sender on `telegram`
- `"*:alice"`: allow sender `alice` on any channel

non_cli_excluded_tools.description:
Tools to exclude from non-CLI channels (e.g. Telegram, Discord).

When a tool is listed here, non-CLI channels will not expose it to the
model in tool specs.

non_cli_natural_language_approval_mode.description:
Natural-language handling mode for non-CLI approval-management commands.

Values:
- `direct` (default): phrases like `授权工具 shell` immediately approve.
- `request_confirm`: phrases create pending requests requiring confirm.
- `disabled`: ignore natural-language approval commands (slash only).

non_cli_natural_language_approval_mode_b.description:
Optional per-channel override for natural-language approval mode.

Keys are channel names (for example: `telegram`, `discord`, `slack`).
Values use the same enum as `non_cli_natural_language_approval_mode`.

Example:
- `telegram = "direct"` for private-chat ergonomics
- `discord = "request_confirm"` for stricter team channels

require_approval_for_medium_risk.description: Require explicit approval for medium-risk shell commands.

shell_env_passthrough.description:
Additional environment variables allowed for shell tool subprocesses.

These names are explicitly allowlisted and merged with the built-in safe
baseline (`PATH`, `HOME`, etc.) after `env_clear()`.

workspace_only.description:
Restrict absolute filesystem paths to workspace-relative references. Default: `true`.
Resolved paths outside the workspace still require `allowed_roots`.

AutonomyLevel.description: How much autonomy the agent has

oneOf.description: Read-only: can observe but not act
Supervised: acts but requires approval for risky operations
Full: autonomous execution within policy bounds

BrowserComputerUseConfig.description:
Computer-use sidecar configuration (`[browser.computer_use]` section).

Delegates OS-level mouse, keyboard, and screenshot actions to a local sidecar.

allow_remote_endpoint.description: Allow remote/public endpoint for computer-use sidecar (default: false)

api_key.description: Optional bearer token for computer-use sidecar

endpoint.description: Sidecar endpoint for computer-use actions (OS-level mouse/keyboard/screenshot)

max_coordinate_x.description: Optional X-axis boundary for coordinate-based actions

max_coordinate_y.description: Optional Y-axis boundary for coordinate-based actions

window_allowlist.description: Optional window title/process allowlist forwarded to sidecar policy

BrowserConfig.description:
Browser automation configuration (`[browser]` section).

Controls the `browser_open` tool and browser automation backends.

allowed_domains.description: Allowed domains for `browser_open` (exact or subdomain match)

enabled.description: Enable `browser_open` tool (opens URLs in the system browser without scraping)

native_chrome_path.description: Optional Chrome/Chromium executable path for rust-native backend

native_webdriver_url.description: WebDriver endpoint URL for rust-native backend (e.g. http://127.0.0.1:9515)

command_logger.description: Enable the command-logger hook (logs tool calls for auditing).

cli.description: Enable the CLI interactive channel. Default: `true`.

message_timeout_secs.description:
Base timeout in seconds for processing a single channel message (LLM + tools).
Runtime uses this as a per-turn budget that scales with tool-loop depth
(up to 4x, capped) so one slow/retried model call does not consume the
entire conversation budget.
Default: 300s for on-device LLMs (Ollama) which are slower than cloud APIs.

wati.description: WATI WhatsApp Business API channel configuration.

whatsapp.description: WhatsApp channel configuration (Cloud API or Web mode).

ClassificationRule.description: A single classification rule mapping message patterns to a model hint.

max_length.description: Only match if message length <= N chars.

min_length.description: Only match if message length >= N chars.

priority.description: Higher priority rules are checked first.

ClawdTalkConfig.description: Configuration for ClawdTalk channel from config.toml

from_number.description: Phone number to call from (E.164 format)

token.description: Cloudflare Tunnel token (from Zero Trust dashboard)

ComposioConfig.description:
Composio managed OAuth tools integration (`[composio]` section).

Provides access to 1000+ OAuth-connected tools via the Composio platform.

api_key.description: Composio API key (stored encrypted when secrets.encrypt = true)

enabled.description: Enable Composio integration for 1000+ OAuth tools

entity_id.description: Default entity ID for multi-user setups

CoordinationConfig.description:
Delegate coordination runtime configuration (`[coordination]` section).

Controls typed delegate message-bus integration used by `delegate` and
`delegate_coordination_status` tools.

enabled.description: Enable delegate coordination tracing/runtime bus integration.

lead_agent.description: Logical lead-agent identity used as coordinator sender/recipient.

max_context_entries.description: Maximum retained shared-context entries (`ContextPatch` state keys).

max_inbox_messages_per_agent.description: Maximum retained inbox messages per registered agent.

max_seen_message_ids.description: Maximum retained dedupe window size for processed message IDs.

CostConfig.description: Cost tracking and budget enforcement configuration (`[cost]` section).

allow_override.description: Allow requests to exceed budget with --override flag (default: false)

warn_at_percent.description: Warn when spending reaches this percentage of limit (default: 80)

max_run_history.description: Maximum number of historical cron run records to retain. Default: `50`.

health_url.description: Optional URL to check tunnel health

start_command.description:
Command template to start the tunnel. Use {port} and {host} placeholders.
Example: "bore local {port} --to bore.pub"

url_pattern.description: Optional regex to extract public URL from command stdout

DelegateAgentConfig.description: Configuration for a delegate sub-agent used by the `delegate` tool.

agentic.description: Enable agentic sub-agent mode (multi-turn tool-call loop).

allowed_tools.description: Allowlist of tool names available to the sub-agent in agentic mode.

max_depth.description: Max recursion depth for nested delegation

max_iterations.description: Maximum tool-call iterations in agentic mode.

system_prompt.description: Optional system prompt for the sub-agent

DingTalkConfig.description: DingTalk configuration for Stream Mode messaging

allowed_users.description: Allowed user IDs (staff IDs). Empty = deny all, "*" = allow all

client_id.description: Client ID (AppKey) from DingTalk developer console

client_secret.description: Client Secret (AppSecret) from DingTalk developer console

allowed_users.description: Allowed Discord user IDs. Empty = deny all.

bot_token.description: Discord bot token (from Discord Developer Portal).

guild_id.description: Optional guild (server) ID to restrict the bot to a single guild.

listen_to_bots.description:
When true, process messages from other bots (not just humans).
The bot still ignores its own messages to prevent feedback loops.

mention_only.description:
When true, only respond to messages that @-mention the bot.
Other messages in the guild are silently ignored.

allowed_workspace_roots.description: Optional workspace root allowlist for Docker mount validation.

cpu_limit.description: Optional CPU limit (`None` = no explicit limit).

image.description: Runtime image used to execute shell commands.

memory_limit_mb.description: Optional memory limit in MB (`None` = no explicit limit).

allowed_senders.description: Allowed sender addresses/domains (empty = deny all, ["*"] = allow all)

idle_timeout_secs.description:
IDLE timeout in seconds before re-establishing connection (default: 1740 = 29 minutes)
RFC 2177 recommends clients restart IDLE every 29 minutes

EmbeddingRouteConfig.description:
Route an embedding hint to a specific provider + model.

```toml
[[embedding_routes]]
hint = "semantic"
provider = "openai"
model = "text-embedding-3-small"
dimensions = 1536

[memory]
embedding_model = "hint:semantic"
```

api_key.description: Optional API key override for this route's provider

dimensions.description: Optional embedding dimension override for this route

model.description: Embedding model to use with that provider

require_otp_to_resume.description: Require a valid OTP before resume operations.

state_file.description: File path used to persist estop state.

allowed_users.description: Allowed user IDs or union IDs (empty = deny all, "*" = allow all)

app_id.description: App ID from Feishu developer console

app_secret.description: App Secret from Feishu developer console

draft_update_interval_ms.description: Minimum interval between streaming draft edits (milliseconds).

encrypt_key.description: Encrypt key for webhook message decryption (optional)

max_draft_edits.description: Maximum number of draft edits per message before finalizing.

port.description:
HTTP port for webhook mode only. Must be set when receive_mode = "webhook".
Not required (and ignored) for websocket mode.

GatewayConfig.description:
Gateway server configuration (`[gateway]` section).

Controls the HTTP gateway for webhook and pairing endpoints.

allow_public_bind.description: Allow binding to non-localhost without a tunnel (default: false)

idempotency_max_keys.description: Maximum distinct idempotency keys retained in memory.

pair_rate_limit_per_minute.description: Max `/pair` requests per minute per client key.

paired_tokens.description: Paired bearer tokens (managed automatically, not user-edited)

rate_limit_max_keys.description: Maximum distinct client keys tracked by gateway rate limiter maps.

require_pairing.description: Require pairing before accepting requests (default: true)

trust_forwarded_headers.description:
Trust proxy-forwarded client IP headers (`X-Forwarded-For`, `X-Real-IP`).
Disabled by default; enable only behind a trusted reverse proxy.

webhook_rate_limit_per_minute.description: Max `/webhook` requests per minute per client key.

GoalLoopConfig.description: Configuration for the autonomous goal loop engine (`[goal_loop]`).

channel.description: Optional channel to deliver goal events to (e.g. "lark", "telegram").

interval_minutes.description: Interval in minutes between goal loop cycles. Default: `10`.

max_steps_per_cycle.description: Maximum steps to execute per cycle. Default: `3`.

step_timeout_secs.description: Timeout in seconds for a single step execution. Default: `120`.

target.description: Optional recipient/chat_id for goal event delivery.

allowed_sender_ids.description:
Sender IDs that always trigger group replies.

These IDs bypass mention gating in group chats, but do not bypass the
channel-level inbound allowlist (`allowed_users` / equivalents).

mode.description:
Optional explicit trigger mode.

If omitted, channel-specific legacy behavior is used for compatibility.

GroupReplyMode.description: Group-chat reply trigger mode for channels that support mention gating.

oneOf.description: Reply only when the bot is explicitly @-mentioned in group chats.
Reply to every message in group chats.

HardwareConfig.description: Wizard-driven hardware configuration for physical world interaction.

workspace_datasheets.description: Enable workspace datasheet RAG (index PDF schematics for AI pin lookups)

HeartbeatConfig.description: Heartbeat configuration for periodic health pings (`[heartbeat]` section).

interval_minutes.description: Interval in minutes between heartbeat pings. Default: `30`.

message.description: Optional fallback task text when `HEARTBEAT.md` has no task entries.

target.description: Optional delivery channel for heartbeat output (for example: `telegram`).

to.description: Optional delivery recipient/chat identifier (required when `target` is set).

enabled.description:
Enable lifecycle hook execution.

Hooks run in-process with the same privileges as the main runtime.
Keep enabled hook handlers narrowly scoped and auditable.

HttpRequestConfig.description:
HTTP request tool configuration (`[http_request]` section).

Deny-by-default: if `allowed_domains` is empty, all HTTP requests are rejected.

allowed_domains.description: Allowed domains for HTTP requests (exact or subdomain match)

max_response_size.description: Maximum response size in bytes (default: 1MB, 0 = unlimited)

user_agent.description: User-Agent string sent with HTTP requests (env: ZEROCLAW_HTTP_REQUEST_USER_AGENT)

allowed_contacts.description: Allowed iMessage contacts (phone numbers or email addresses). Empty = deny all.

IdentityConfig.description:
Identity format configuration (`[identity]` section).

Supports `"openclaw"` (default) or `"aieos"` identity documents.

aieos_inline.description: Inline AIEOS JSON (alternative to file path)

aieos_path.description: Path to AIEOS JSON file (relative to workspace)

username.description: Username (defaults to nickname if not set)

LarkConfig.description:
Lark/Feishu configuration for messaging integration.
Lark is the international version; Feishu is the Chinese version.

allowed_users.description: Allowed user IDs or union IDs (empty = deny all, "*" = allow all)

app_id.description: App ID from Lark/Feishu developer console

app_secret.description: App Secret from Lark/Feishu developer console

draft_update_interval_ms.description: Minimum interval (ms) between draft message edits. Default: 3000.

encrypt_key.description: Encrypt key for webhook message decryption (optional)

max_draft_edits.description: Maximum number of edits per draft message before stopping updates.

mention_only.description:
When true, only respond to messages that @-mention the bot in groups.
Direct messages are always processed.

port.description:
HTTP port for webhook mode only. Must be set when receive_mode = "webhook".
Not required (and ignored) for websocket mode.

use_feishu.description: Whether to use the Feishu (Chinese) endpoint instead of Lark (International)

LarkReceiveMode.description:
How ZeroClaw receives events from Feishu / Lark.

- `websocket` (default) — persistent WSS long-connection; no public URL required.
- `webhook`             — HTTP callback server; requires a public HTTPS endpoint.

allowed_senders.description: Allowed sender handles (phone numbers) or "*" for all

from_phone.description: Phone number to send from (E.164 format)

signing_secret.description: Webhook signing secret for signature verification

access_token.description: Matrix access token for the bot account.

allowed_users.description: Allowed Matrix user IDs. Empty = deny all.

mention_only.description: When true, only respond to direct rooms, explicit @-mentions, or replies to bot messages.

room_id.description: Matrix room ID to listen in (e.g. `"!abc123:matrix.org"`).

allowed_users.description: Allowed Mattermost user IDs. Empty = deny all.

channel_id.description: Optional channel ID to restrict the bot to a single channel.

mention_only.description:
When true, only respond to messages that @-mention the bot.
Other messages in the channel are silently ignored.

thread_replies.description:
When true (default), replies thread on the original post.
When false, replies go to the channel root.

McpServerConfig.description: Configuration for a single external MCP server.

command.description: Executable to spawn for stdio transport.

env.description: Optional environment variables for stdio transport.

headers.description: Optional HTTP headers for HTTP/SSE transports.

name.description: Display name used as a tool prefix (`<server>__<tool>`).

tool_timeout_secs.description: Optional per-call timeout in seconds (hard capped in validation).

McpTransport.description: Transport type for MCP server connections.

oneOf.description: Spawn a local process and communicate over stdin/stdout.
Connect via HTTP POST.
Connect via HTTP + Server-Sent Events.

archive_after_days.description: Archive daily/session files older than this many days

auto_hydrate.description: Auto-hydrate from MEMORY_SNAPSHOT.md when brain.db is missing

auto_save.description: Auto-save user-stated conversation input to memory (assistant output is excluded)

backend.description:
"sqlite" | "lucid" | "postgres" | "qdrant" | "markdown" | "none" (`none` = explicit no-op memory)

`postgres` requires `[storage.provider.config]` with `db_url` (`dbURL` alias supported).
`qdrant` uses `[memory.qdrant]` config or `QDRANT_URL` env var.

chunk_max_tokens.description: Max tokens per chunk for document splitting

conversation_retention_days.description: For sqlite backend: prune conversation rows older than this many days

embedding_cache_size.description: Max embedding cache entries before LRU eviction

keyword_weight.description: Weight for keyword BM25 in hybrid search (0.0–1.0)

min_relevance_score.description:
Minimum hybrid score (0.0–1.0) for a memory to be included in context.
Memories scoring below this threshold are dropped to prevent irrelevant
context from bleeding into conversations. Default: 0.4

purge_after_days.description: Purge archived files older than this many days

qdrant.description:
Configuration for Qdrant vector database backend.
Only used when `backend = "qdrant"`.

response_cache_enabled.description: Enable LLM response caching to avoid paying for duplicate prompts

response_cache_max_entries.description: Max number of cached responses before LRU eviction (default: 5000)

response_cache_ttl_minutes.description: TTL in minutes for cached responses (default: 60)

snapshot_enabled.description: Enable periodic export of core memories to MEMORY_SNAPSHOT.md

sqlite_open_timeout_secs.description:
For sqlite backend: max seconds to wait when opening the DB (e.g. file locked).
None = wait indefinitely (default). Recommended max: 300.

vector_weight.description: Weight for vector similarity in hybrid search (0.0–1.0)

ModelProviderConfig.description: Named provider profile definition compatible with Codex app-server style config.

base_url.description: Optional base URL for OpenAI-compatible endpoints.

name.description: Optional provider type/name override (e.g. "openai", "openai-codex", or custom profile id).

requires_openai_auth.description: If true, load OpenAI auth material (OPENAI_API_KEY or ~/.codex/auth.json).

ModelRouteConfig.description:
Route a task hint to a specific provider + model.

```toml
[[model_routes]]
hint = "reasoning"
provider = "openrouter"
model = "anthropic/claude-opus-4-20250514"

[[model_routes]]
hint = "fast"
provider = "groq"
model = "llama-3.3-70b-versatile"
```

Usage: pass `hint:reasoning` as the model parameter to route the request.

api_key.description: Optional API key override for this route's provider

max_tokens.description:
Optional max_tokens override for this route.
When set, provider requests cap output tokens to this value.

model.description: Model to use with that provider

provider.description: Provider to route to (must match a known provider name)

allow_remote_fetch.description: Allow fetching remote image URLs (http/https). Disabled by default.

max_image_size_mb.description: Maximum image payload size in MiB before base64 encoding.

max_images.description: Maximum number of image attachments accepted per request.

NextcloudTalkConfig.description: Nextcloud Talk bot configuration (webhook receive + OCS send API).

allowed_users.description: Allowed Nextcloud actor IDs (`[]` = deny all, `"*"` = allow all).

app_token.description: Bot app token used for OCS API bearer auth.

webhook_secret.description:
Shared secret for webhook signature verification.

Can also be set via `ZEROCLAW_NEXTCLOUD_TALK_WEBHOOK_SECRET`.

allowed_node_ids.description:
Allowlist of remote node IDs for `node.describe`/`node.invoke`.
Empty means "no explicit allowlist" (accept all IDs).

auth_token.description:
Optional extra shared token for node-control API calls.
When set, clients must send this value in `X-Node-Control-Token`.

NonCliNaturalLanguageApprovalMode.description: Natural-language behavior for non-CLI approval-management commands.

oneOf.description:
Do not treat natural-language text as approval-management commands.
Operators must use explicit slash commands.

Natural-language approval phrases create a pending request that must be
confirmed with a request ID.

Natural-language approval phrases directly approve the named tool.

This keeps private-chat workflows simple while still requiring a human
sender and passing the same approver allowlist checks as slash commands.

NostrConfig.description: Nostr channel configuration (NIP-04 + NIP-17 private messages)

allowed_pubkeys.description: Allowed sender public keys (hex or npub). Empty = deny all, "*" = allow all

private_key.description: Private key in hex or nsec bech32 format

relays.description: Relay URLs (wss://). Defaults to popular public relays if omitted.

otel_endpoint.description: OTLP endpoint (e.g. "http://localhost:4318"). Only used when backend = "otel".

otel_service_name.description: Service name reported to the OTel collector. Defaults to "zeroclaw".

runtime_trace_mode.description:
Runtime trace storage mode: "none" | "rolling" | "full".
Controls whether model replies and tool-call diagnostics are persisted.

runtime_trace_path.description: Runtime trace file path. Relative paths are resolved under workspace_dir.

OtpChallengeDelivery.description: Channel delivery mode for OTP challenges.

oneOf.description: Send OTP challenge in direct message/private channel.
Send OTP challenge in thread where supported.
Send OTP challenge as ephemeral message where supported.

cache_valid_secs.description: Reuse window for recently validated OTP codes.

challenge_delivery.description: Delivery mode for OTP challenge prompts in chat channels.

challenge_max_attempts.description: Maximum OTP attempts allowed per challenge.

challenge_timeout_secs.description: Maximum time a challenge remains valid, in seconds.

enabled.description: Enable OTP gating. Defaults to enabled.

gated_domains.description: Explicit domain patterns gated by OTP.

oneOf.description: Time-based one-time password (RFC 6238).
Future method for paired-device confirmations.
Future method for local CLI challenge prompts.

PeripheralBoardConfig.description: Configuration for a single peripheral board (e.g. STM32, RPi GPIO).

PeripheralsConfig.description:
Peripheral board integration configuration (`[peripherals]` section).

Boards become agent tools when enabled.

datasheet_dir.description:
Path to datasheet docs (relative to workspace) for RAG retrieval.
Place .md/.txt files named by board (e.g. nucleo-f401re.md, rpi-gpio.md).

enabled.description: Enable peripheral support (boards become agent tools)

enable_perplexity_filter.description: Enable probabilistic adversarial suffix filtering before provider calls.

min_prompt_chars.description: Minimum input length before running the perplexity filter.

perplexity_threshold.description: Character-class bigram perplexity threshold for anomaly blocking.

suffix_window_chars.description: Number of trailing characters sampled for suffix anomaly scoring.

symbol_ratio_threshold.description: Minimum punctuation ratio in the sampled suffix required to block.

enabled.description:
Override the plugin's enabled state. If absent, the plugin is enabled
unless it is bundled-and-disabled-by-default.

PluginsConfig.description:
Plugin system configuration (`[plugins]` section).

Controls plugin discovery, loading, and per-plugin settings.
Mirrors OpenClaw's `plugins` config block.

Example:
```toml
[plugins]
enabled = true
allow = ["hello-world"]

[plugins.entries.hello-world]
enabled = true

[plugins.entries.hello-world.config]
greeting = "Howdy"
```

allow.description:
Allowlist — if non-empty, only plugins with these IDs are loaded.
An empty list means all discovered plugins are eligible.

deny.description: Denylist — plugins with these IDs are never loaded, even if in the allowlist.

enabled.description: Master switch — set to `false` to disable all plugin loading. Default: `true`.

load_paths.description:
Extra directories to scan for plugins (in addition to the standard locations).
Standard locations: `<binary_dir>/extensions/`, `~/.zeroclaw/extensions/`,
`<workspace>/.zeroclaw/extensions/`.

oneOf.description:
Default behavior: `/chat/completions` first, optional `/responses`
fallback when supported.
Responses-first behavior: call `/responses` directly.

reasoning_level.description:
Optional reasoning level override for providers that support explicit levels
(e.g. OpenAI Codex `/responses` reasoning effort).

ProxyConfig.description: Proxy configuration for outbound HTTP/HTTPS/SOCKS5 traffic (`[proxy]` section).

all_proxy.description: Fallback proxy URL for all schemes.

enabled.description: Enable proxy support for selected scope.

http_proxy.description: Proxy URL for HTTP requests (supports http, https, socks5, socks5h).

https_proxy.description: Proxy URL for HTTPS requests (supports http, https, socks5, socks5h).

no_proxy.description: No-proxy bypass list. Same format as NO_PROXY.

ProxyScope.description: Proxy application scope — determines which outbound traffic uses the proxy.

oneOf.description: Use system environment proxy variables only.
Apply proxy to all ZeroClaw-managed HTTP traffic (default).
Apply proxy only to explicitly listed service selectors.

QQConfig.description: QQ Official Bot configuration (Tencent QQ Bot SDK)

allowed_users.description: Allowed user IDs. Empty = deny all, "*" = allow all

app_id.description: App ID from QQ Bot developer console

app_secret.description: App Secret from QQ Bot developer console

QQReceiveMode.description: QQ Official Bot configuration (Tencent QQ Bot SDK)

QdrantConfig.description:
Memory backend configuration (`[memory]` section).

Controls conversation memory storage, embeddings, hybrid search, response caching,
and memory snapshot/hydration.
Configuration for Qdrant vector database backend (`[memory.qdrant]`).
Used when `[memory].backend = "qdrant"`.

api_key.description:
Optional API key for Qdrant Cloud or secured instances.
Falls back to `QDRANT_API_KEY` env var if not set.

collection.description:
Qdrant collection name for storing memories.
Falls back to `QDRANT_COLLECTION` env var, or default "zeroclaw_memories".

url.description:
Qdrant server URL (e.g. "http://localhost:6333").
Falls back to `QDRANT_URL` env var if not set.

QueryClassificationConfig.description:
Automatic query classification — classifies user messages by keyword/pattern
and routes to the appropriate model hint. Disabled by default.

rules.description: Classification rules evaluated in priority order.

ReliabilityConfig.description:
Reliability and supervision configuration (`[reliability]` section).

Controls provider retries, fallback chains, API key rotation, and channel restart backoff.

api_keys.description:
Additional API keys for round-robin rotation on rate-limit (429) errors.
The primary `api_key` is always tried first; these are extras.

model_fallbacks.description:
Per-model fallback chains. When a model fails, try these alternatives in order.
Example: `{ "claude-opus-4-20250514" = ["claude-sonnet-4-20250514", "gpt-4o"] }`

Compatibility behavior: keys matching configured provider names are treated
as provider-scoped remap chains during provider fallback.

provider_backoff_ms.description: Base backoff (ms) for provider retry delay.

provider_retries.description: Retries per provider before failing over.

scheduler_retries.description: Max retries for cron job execution attempts.

ResearchPhaseConfig.description:
Research phase configuration (`[research]` section).

When enabled, the agent proactively gathers information using tools
before generating its main response. This creates a "thinking" phase
where the agent explores the codebase, searches memory, or fetches
external data to inform its answer.

```toml
[research]
enabled = true
trigger = "keywords"
keywords = ["find", "search", "check", "investigate"]
max_iterations = 5
show_progress = true
```

max_iterations.description: Maximum tool call iterations during research phase.

min_message_length.description: Minimum message length to trigger research (when `trigger = "length"`).

show_progress.description: Show detailed progress during research (tool calls, results).

system_prompt_prefix.description:
Custom system prompt prefix for research phase.
If empty, uses default research instructions.

oneOf.description: Never trigger research phase.
Always trigger research phase before responding.
Trigger when message contains configured keywords.
Trigger when message exceeds minimum length.
Trigger when message contains a question mark.

max_cpu_time_seconds.description: Maximum CPU time in seconds per command

max_memory_mb.description: Maximum memory in MB per command

reasoning_enabled.description:
Global reasoning override for providers that expose explicit controls.
- `None`: provider default behavior
- `Some(true)`: request reasoning/thinking when supported
- `Some(false)`: disable reasoning/thinking when supported

reasoning_level.description:
Deprecated compatibility alias for `[provider].reasoning_level`.
- Canonical key: `provider.reasoning_level`
- Legacy key accepted for compatibility: `runtime.reasoning_level`
- When both are set, provider-level value wins.

oneOf.description: Auto-detect best available (default)
Landlock (Linux kernel LSM, native)
Firejail (user-space sandbox)
Bubblewrap (user namespaces)
Docker container isolation
No sandboxing (application-layer only)

SchedulerConfig.description: Scheduler configuration for periodic task execution (`[scheduler]` section).

max_concurrent.description: Maximum tasks executed per scheduler polling cycle.

max_tasks.description: Maximum number of persisted scheduled tasks.

encrypt.description: Enable encryption for API keys and tokens in config.toml

SecurityConfig.description: Security configuration for sandboxing, resource limits, and audit logging

otp.description: OTP gating configuration for sensitive actions/domains.

perplexity_filter.description: Lightweight statistical filter for adversarial suffixes (opt-in).

roles.description: Custom security role definitions used for user-level tool authorization.

syscall_anomaly.description: Syscall anomaly detection profile for daemon shell/process execution.

url_access.description: Shared URL access policy for network-enabled tools.

SecurityRoleConfig.description: Custom role definition for user-level authorization.

allowed_tools.description: Explicit allowlist of tools for this role.

denied_tools.description: Explicit denylist of tools for this role.

inherits.description: Optional parent role name used for inheritance.

name.description: Stable role name used by user records.

totp_gated.description: Tool names requiring OTP for this role.

account.description: E.164 phone number of the signal-cli account (e.g. "+1234567890").

allowed_from.description: Allowed sender phone numbers (E.164) or "*" for all.

group_id.description:
Optional group ID to filter messages.
- `None` or omitted: accept all messages (DMs and groups)
- `"dm"`: only accept direct messages
- Specific group ID: only accept messages from that group

http_url.description: Base URL for the signal-cli HTTP daemon (e.g. "http://127.0.0.1:8686").

ignore_attachments.description: Skip messages that are attachment-only (no text body).

allow_scripts.description:
Allow script-like files in skills (`.sh`, `.bash`, `.ps1`, shebang shell files).
Default: `false` (secure by default).

clawhub_token.description:
Optional ClawhHub API token for authenticated skill downloads.
Obtain from https://clawhub.ai after signing in.
Set via config: `clawhub_token = "..."` under `[skills]`.

open_skills_dir.description:
Optional path to a local open-skills repository.
If unset, defaults to `$HOME/open-skills` when enabled.

open_skills_enabled.description:
Enable loading and syncing the community open-skills repository.
Default: `false` (opt-in).

prompt_injection_mode.description:
Controls how skills are injected into the system prompt.
`full` preserves legacy behavior. `compact` keeps context small and loads skills on demand.

oneOf.description: Inline full skill instructions and tool metadata into the system prompt.
Inline only compact skill metadata (name/description/location) and load details on demand.

allowed_users.description: Allowed Slack user IDs. Empty = deny all.

app_token.description: Slack app-level token for Socket Mode (xapp-...).

channel_id.description:
Optional channel ID to restrict the bot to a single channel.
Omit (or set `"*"`) to listen across all accessible channels.

StorageProviderConfig.description: Storage provider backend configuration (e.g. postgres connection details).

connect_timeout_secs.description: Optional connection timeout in seconds for remote providers.

db_url.description:
Connection URL for remote providers.
Accepts legacy aliases: dbURL, database_url, databaseUrl.

tls.description:
Enable TLS for the PostgreSQL connection.

`true` — require TLS (skips certificate verification; suitable for
self-signed certs and most managed databases).
`false` (default) — plain TCP, backward-compatible.

StorageProviderSection.description: Wrapper for the storage provider configuration section.

StreamMode.description: Streaming mode for channels that support progressive message updates.

oneOf.description: No streaming -- send the complete response as a single message (default).
Update a draft message with every flush interval.

alert_on_unknown_syscall.description: Emit anomaly alerts when a syscall appears outside the expected baseline.

baseline_syscalls.description: Expected syscall baseline. Unknown syscall names trigger anomaly when enabled.

log_path.description: Path to syscall anomaly log file (relative to ~/.zeroclaw unless absolute).

max_alerts_per_minute.description: Maximum anomaly alerts emitted per rolling minute (global guardrail).

max_denied_events_per_minute.description: Allowed denied-syscall events per rolling minute before triggering an alert.

max_total_events_per_minute.description: Allowed total syscall telemetry events per rolling minute before triggering an alert.

strict_mode.description: Treat denied syscall lines as anomalies even when syscall is in baseline.

funnel.description: Use Tailscale Funnel (public internet) vs Serve (tailnet only)

allowed_users.description: Allowed Telegram user IDs or usernames. Empty = deny all.

base_url.description:
Optional custom base URL for Telegram-compatible APIs.
Defaults to "https://api.telegram.org" when omitted.
Example for Bale messenger: "https://tapi.bale.ai"

draft_update_interval_ms.description: Minimum interval (ms) between draft message edits to avoid rate limits.

interrupt_on_new_message.description:
When true, a newer Telegram message from the same sender in the same chat
cancels the in-flight request and starts a fresh response with preserved history.

mention_only.description:
When true, only respond to messages that @-mention the bot in groups.
Direct messages are always processed.

stream_mode.description: Streaming mode for progressive response delivery via message edits.

TranscriptionConfig.description: Voice transcription configuration (Whisper API via Groq).

enabled.description: Enable voice transcription for channels that support it.

max_duration_secs.description: Maximum voice duration in seconds (messages longer than this are skipped).

TunnelConfig.description:
Tunnel configuration for exposing the gateway publicly (`[tunnel]` section).

Supported providers: `"none"` (default), `"cloudflare"`, `"tailscale"`, `"ngrok"`, `"custom"`.

UrlAccessConfig.description: Shared URL validation configuration used by network tools.

allow_cidrs.description: Explicit CIDR ranges that bypass private/local-IP blocking.

allow_domains.description:
Explicit domain patterns that bypass private/local-IP blocking.
Supports exact, `*.example.com`, and `*`.

block_private_ip.description: Block private/local IPs and hostnames by default.

WasmCapabilityEscalationMode.description: How to handle invocation capabilities that exceed baseline runtime policy.

oneOf.description: Reject any invocation that asks for capabilities above runtime config.
Automatically clamp invocation capabilities to runtime config ceilings.

WasmConfig.description:
WASM plugin engine configuration (`[wasm]` section).

Controls limits applied to every WASM tool invocation.
Requires the `wasm-tools` compile-time feature to have any effect.

enabled.description:
Enable loading WASM tools from installed skill packages.
Default: `true` (auto-discovers plugins in the skills directory).

fuel_limit.description:
CPU fuel budget per invocation (roughly one unit ≈ one WASM instruction).
Default: 1_000_000_000.

memory_limit_mb.description:
Maximum linear memory per WASM invocation in MiB.
Valid range: 1..=256. Default: `64`.

registry_url.description:
URL of the ZeroMarket (or compatible) registry used by `zeroclaw skill install`.
Default: the public ZeroMarket registry.

WasmModuleHashPolicy.description: Integrity policy for WASM modules pinned by SHA-256 digest.

oneOf.description: Disable module hash validation.
Warn on missing or mismatched hashes, but allow execution.
Require exact hash match before execution.

allow_workspace_read.description: Allow reading files from workspace inside WASM host calls (future-facing).

allow_workspace_write.description: Allow writing files to workspace inside WASM host calls (future-facing).

allowed_hosts.description: Explicit host allowlist for outbound HTTP from WASM modules (future-facing).

memory_limit_mb.description: Memory limit per invocation in MB.

WasmSecurityConfig.description: Security policy controls for WASM runtime hardening.

module_sha256.description: Optional pinned SHA-256 digest map keyed by module name (without `.wasm`).

reject_symlink_modules.description: Reject module files that are symlinks before execution.

reject_symlink_tools_dir.description: Reject `runtime.wasm.tools_dir` when it is itself a symlink.

require_workspace_relative_tools_dir.description: Require `runtime.wasm.tools_dir` to stay workspace-relative and traversal-free.

strict_host_validation.description: Strictly validate host allowlist entries (`host` or `host:port` only).

WatiConfig.description: WATI WhatsApp Business API channel configuration.

allowed_numbers.description: Allowed phone numbers (E.164 format) or "*" for all.

WebFetchConfig.description:
Web fetch tool configuration (`[web_fetch]` section).

Fetches web pages and converts HTML to plain text for LLM consumption.
Domain filtering: `allowed_domains` controls which hosts are reachable (use `["*"]`
for all public hosts). `blocked_domains` takes priority over `allowed_domains`.
If `allowed_domains` is empty, all requests are rejected (deny-by-default).

allowed_domains.description: Allowed domains for web fetch (exact or subdomain match; `["*"]` = all public hosts)

api_key.description: Optional provider API key (required for provider = "firecrawl")

api_url.description: Optional provider API URL override (for self-hosted providers)

blocked_domains.description: Blocked domains (exact or subdomain match; always takes priority over allowed_domains)

enabled.description: Enable `web_fetch` tool for fetching web page content

max_response_size.description: Maximum response size in bytes (default: 500KB, plain text is much smaller than raw HTML)

user_agent.description: User-Agent string sent with fetch requests (env: ZEROCLAW_WEB_FETCH_USER_AGENT)

api_key.description: Generic provider API key (used by firecrawl and as fallback for brave)

api_url.description: Optional provider API URL override (for self-hosted providers)

brave_api_key.description: Brave Search API key (required if provider is "brave")

provider.description: Search provider: "duckduckgo" (free, no API key) or "brave" (requires API key)

user_agent.description: User-Agent string sent with search requests (env: ZEROCLAW_WEB_SEARCH_USER_AGENT)

port.description: Port to listen on for incoming webhooks.

secret.description: Optional shared secret for webhook signature verification.

WhatsAppConfig.description:
WhatsApp channel configuration (Cloud API or Web mode).

Set `phone_number_id` for Cloud API mode, or `session_path` for Web mode.

access_token.description: Access token from Meta Business Suite (Cloud API mode)

allowed_numbers.description: Allowed phone numbers (E.164 format: +1234567890) or "*" for all

app_secret.description:
App secret from Meta Business Suite (for webhook signature verification)
Can also be set via `ZEROCLAW_WHATSAPP_APP_SECRET` environment variable
Only used in Cloud API mode

pair_code.description:
Custom pair code for linking (Web mode, optional)
Leave empty to let WhatsApp generate one

pair_phone.description:
Phone number for pair code linking (Web mode, optional)
Format: country code + number (e.g., "15551234567")
If not set, QR code pairing will be used

phone_number_id.description: Phone number ID from Meta Business API (Cloud API mode)

session_path.description:
Session database path for WhatsApp Web client (Web mode)
When set, enables native WhatsApp Web mode with wa-rs

verify_token.description:
Webhook verify token (you define this, Meta sends it back for verification)
Only used in Cloud API mode
